fields.description:
## Context
The shared review kernel's impact() (src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/decide.py:111) computes impact = mean(max(prod_impact,debt_impact), blast_radius, likelihood, reversibility), each axis mapped none/local/low/easy=0.33 .. high/system/hard=1.0. This model was implicitly shaped by PLAN-REVIEW and mis-measures CODE-REVIEW findings.

Evidence (same model, two populations):
- PLAN-REVIEW would-block (n=921): prod_impact high=59%, blast module+=98%, likelihood high=78%, reversibility moderate/hard=84% -> impact 0.78+. Fits: plan-review findings are production/design-CONSEQUENCE shaped.
- CODE-REVIEW (n=31 live re-runs): prod_impact none/low=96% (0% high), reversibility easy=93%, blast local/module, debt_impact never high -> impact capped ~0.25-0.60. A feature silently dead in prod (untested wiring) scores prod_impact=none, impact=0.415 — overlapping the doc-nit band, so NO threshold separates landmines from nits.

## Why diverge (do NOT share the plan-review model — sibling of c34d, kept separate)
The two gates measure different things with different needs:
- Plan-review impact = production/design consequence of shipping a flawed PLAN. prod_impact / blast / likelihood / reversibility-of-the-approach are the right axes.
- Code-review impact = long-term MAINTAINABILITY / latent-regression risk of a DIFF. Dominant axis should be tech-debt / detection-gap, NOT immediate production blast. reversibility-of-the-change is not merely irrelevant but INVERTED here — a detection gap that is easy to reverse yet easy to miss (a missing test) is HIGH risk, but the model scores easy-reversibility as LOW impact. 93% of code-review findings hit that floor.
Forcing both through one production-severity model means code-review can never score its own high-value findings above its own nits.

## Directions (design later)
- A code-review-specific impact model: debt/maintainability severity primary (allow high/critical); a detection-gap / latent-regression axis (does this create a future SILENT failure?); blast measured on the FUTURE-change surface (how much future work does this booby-trap); DROP or invert reversibility for detection-gap findings.
- Keep the shared decide.py kernel but DISPATCH impact() per-gate (mirrors how threshold_for already dispatches per-gate in criteria/model.py), so plan-review keeps its production-severity model unchanged and code-review gets a maintainability model.
- Re-derive/validate from the code-review corpus once LLM blocking is trialed.

## Relationship
Sibling of c34d (neat-sooty-termite = PLAN-REVIEW impact aggregation / mean-dilution). Deliberately SEPARATE tickets: the models diverge. This ticket is the code-review-gate impact model; c34d is the plan-review-gate aggregation.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Parking-lot idea; not scheduled. Revisit alongside introducing any code-review LLM blocking (the DET new-behavior-without-test gate is the near-term substitute that sidesteps the scalar).
